text.div: I live with type 1 diabetes, and wear an automated insulin delivery system. I have no known allergies to medication.
I live with type 1 diabetes.
I have no known medication related allergies. I'm mildly allergic to apple, birch pollen, and some nuts.
I use insulin (NovoRapid) for diabetes, and rosuvastatin and ezetimibe for diabetes related hypercholesterolemia.

entry.display: Insulin NovoRapid 100 units/ml. My prescription is for 50 - 100 iU/d. I usually consume 40 to 70 iU per day.
Rosuvastatin 10 mg. Started with 5mg in 2022, dose set to 10mg late 2024.

Ezetimibe 10 mg. I know I should be taking both rosuvastatin and ezetimibe in the evening, but I take them in the morning, because that's when I remember to take them. My doctor knows this, and approves.

text.div: Hepatitis A+B (2011), tetanus (2015), typhoid (2011) and yellow fever (2007). 4 covid-19 vaccines, last in November 2023. Occasional influenza vaccines.

There's nothing I consider exceptional in my diagnostic tests or laboratory test results. All are in normal ranges, except for HbA1c, for which the latest result was 49 mmol/mol. Latest Chol LDL was 1.3 mmol/l.

text.div:
I generally trust my automated insulin delivery system. If possible, I prefer to wear it even when unconsious, rather than having someone administer insulin by a fixed or estimated scale.

I'm driven and have many things still to achieve, and prefer to be resuscitated if there is a chance for me to become capable of interacting with the world.

In case of death, I am willing to donate my organs for whatever purpose.

Since I have a history of using automated insulin delivery systems developed by an open-source development community, in the case of my passing away I'd like it to be communicated whether the use of such a system played any role in the event.

Regarding my data, including all information in this document, I consent to it being used for my treatment, including with AI based decision support tools, and to it being stored to electronic health record systems. I also consent to this information being used for training of healthcare professionals and for machine learning training in de-identified form only.

I have no disabilities. I don't think I have any real records of functional status assessments performed by a clinician. Personally, I feel I'm in relatively good health and in relatively good condition. I can do what I want to do.
I have had, and still occasionally have problems with mobility of my right shoulder. I've suffered a pulmonary embolism event in 2022. I have had many of my bones broken in various accidents.

text.div: Regarding my type 1 diabetes, I consider myself an independent and empowered patient. Diabetes forces me to take care of myself, but I still often prioritize the things I want to achieve in life over my own short-term health and well-being.

My primary contact to healthcare is through occupational healthcare, currently organized by Terveystalo, previously by Mehiläinen. For diabetes related things I turn to my endocrinologist, whom I nowadays meet every other year.
I've never smoked. I consume alcohol in moderation, less than once a month and never more than 6 servings at a time.
